column3: DIMMs are the larger memory-module form factor commonly used in desktops and many servers.
column4:
Why

The acronym matters because Objective 3.3 explicitly compares RAM form factors.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.30; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.3
column9: A+::220-1201::3.3 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::RAMCharacteristics Cloze HighYield RAM FormFactors Source::Messer-v170

column2: SODIMM stands for {{c1::Small Outline Dual In-line Memory Module}}.
column3: SODIMMs are smaller modules commonly used in laptops and compact systems.
column4:
Why

SODIMM recall supports the form-factor comparison in Objective 3.3 and helps distinguish laptop memory from desktop memory.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.30; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.3
column9: A+::220-1201::3.3 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::RAMCharacteristics Cloze HighYield RAM FormFactors Source::Messer-v170

column3: DDR generation still matters for compatibility; a replacement module must match the system's supported DDR generation.
column4:
Why

The acronym recall is distinct from the replacement decision in 3.3-B003, which tests DDR generation compatibility.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 pp.30-31; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.3
column9: A+::220-1201::3.3 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::RAMCharacteristics Cloze RAM DDR Source::Messer-v170

column3: ECC RAM can detect and correct certain memory errors when the platform supports it.
column4:
Why

The acronym recall supports the Objective 3.3 comparison between ECC and non-ECC memory.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.31; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.3
column9: A+::220-1201::3.3 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::RAMCharacteristics Cloze HighYield RAM ECC Source::Messer-v170
